heading: Vendor support to clandestine activities
label: 430e Vendor support to clandestine activities
notes: Implementation Deadline and Reports, , , provided that:Pub. L. 119–60, div. A, title XVI, § 1621(b)139 Stat. 1181Dec. 18, 2025“(1).—Implementation deadline and certificationNot later than one year after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Secretary of Defense shall—Dec. 18, 2025“(A)implement the requirements of , United States Code, as added by subsection (a) of this section; andsection 430e of title 10“(B)submit to the congressional defense committees [Committees on Armed Services and Appropriations of the Senate and the House of Representatives], the Select Committee on Intelligence of the Senate, and the Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence of the House of Representatives a certification that such requirements have been implemented.“(2).—Submission of planNot later than 120 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Defense shall—“(A)submit to the committees described in paragraph (1)(B) a report containing the plan to implement the requirements of such section 430e; and“(B)provide to such committees a briefing with respect to such plan.“(3).—Progress reportNot later than 270 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Defense shall provide to the committees described in paragraph (1)(B) a briefing describing the progress of the Secretary towards implementing the requirements of such section 430e.”

children.heading: .—Oversight, Deconfliction, and Risk Assessment Capability
children.label: a .—Oversight, Deconfliction, and Risk Assessment Capability

children.text: enable the Department of Defense to perform oversight, deconfliction, and risk assessments of past, ongoing, or planned clandestine activity involving support from commercial vendors, including all subcontractors; and

children.label: A
children.text: identify potential conflicts between clandestine activities;

children.label: B
children.text: identify the need for additional coordination with respect to clandestine activities; and

children.label: C
children.text: conduct aggregate risk assessments of clandestine activities involving support from commercial vendors; and1So in original.1

Notwithstanding subsection (a), if the Secretary of Defense determines that information concerning a commercial vendor should not be used by the centralized technical capability required by subsection (a) due to operational, counterintelligence, or other national security concerns, the Secretary may exclude such information from use by such centralized technical capability.

children.label: A
children.text: The number of commercial vendors with respect to which the Secretary made the determination under paragraph (1).

children.label: B
children.text: A description of the types of activities supported by such commercial vendors.

children.label: C
children.text: The rationale for excluding the information concerning such commercial vendors from such capability.
deconflict the use of commercial vendors in support of clandestine activities of the Department of Defense; and
assess operational risk and counterintelligence exposure attributable to the use of commercial vendors in support of clandestine activities of the Department of Defense.

children.heading: .—Clandestine Activity Defined
children.label: d .—Clandestine Activity Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “clandestine activity” means any activity where it is intended that the role of the United States Government will not be apparent or acknowledged publicly.
